Latest results show](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/5849523771670528) lots of 'expected cli option doesn't exist' kind of errors, as well as...did I mention I don't like using gpg in automation :grin:   What's my next step/fix needed here?

https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6532738312306688?command=integration_test#L1004-L1029

Great work everybody! #2126 has been merged, so we might be green after another rebase :crossed_fingers:

Rebased, and fingers-crossed.  Assuming it's green, I do want to do some testing of the Bors support on this PR:

- [x] Attempt to enable unit-testing during gate
- [x] Verify `bors try` functions (simulated merge)
- [x] Verify Cirrus-CI works behaves predictably with `bors try`
- [x] Confirm optional stuff is treated as optional.
- [x] Begin work on PR to add some basic docs for bors
:clap: [all green, thanks everyone!](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/5911210843439104) :clap:

bors.toml: not found
Awesome! @cevich, once you feel comfortable to merge this, please remove the `WIP:`.
@vrothberg yep will do.

okay, we're good to go here.  @rhatdan [the test-merge I did above log is here if you want to peek.](https://github.com/containers/buildah/runs/419687894).  If everything looks good, give it a `bors r+` and that should cause this (and #1848) to be tested and then merge (if green).  I'll start in on a docs-update PR for bors-ng.

## try

@cevich is this ready to review and merge?

@rhatdan yes ready to go.

Note:  I'm not sure what the 'TIMED OUT' message is from bors, since clearly (above that) my 'bors try' passed all tests (green check-mark).  It's possible if you `bors r+` this, we will hit something similar.  But I consider this growing-pains, and will address as needed.
bors r+
/me crosses fingers
We can see status here: https://app.bors.tech/batches/45255 still says `running` but (from the green check-mark) it should be finished and merged.  So I suspect we're about to get a timeout error.

:laughing: always one step forward and two steps back :laughing: 

This seems like a flake of some sort.  Going to rebase and try again.

bors r+
Nnooooooooooooo :cry:
bors r-
# Canceled

@rhatdan I was just reviewing the 'bors try' result (notice the red X).  There's a problem, and I wonder if we can even use bors-ng :disappointed:  It's not considering the status of ***ALL*** the `cirrus-ci/required/*` tests, it's picking one that passed and saying "good enough".  I doubt that's what we want :confounded:

Sorry, this is the 'bors try' I'm concerned about: https://github.com/containers/buildah/runs/425890269

Notice after that it posted a comment:  Build succeeded
Ok, sorry about that, I thought we were finally done.  I want travis gone ASAP since it is holding all of the PRs up waiting for it to decide to start.

No it's my fault as well, I should have immediately updated to WIP.

Yeah, you and me both, I was really hoping we were good to go here :cry:

Damnit! `*sigh*` okay.  I'm willing to take this to the upstream bors-ng community, maybe it's a bug, maybe the `bors.toml` is miss-configured.  it's not the end of the world.  It's better to find this problem early rather than late.

...oh, I've got an idea.  Since it only wants to pick from a limited set of required status, we could do the same as we do on libpod: Have a 'success' task which depends on everything that's required.  That should be good-enough for now, and let this PR get merged while I followup with upstream bors-ng.

Sorry @rhatdan for all the "almost" and "one more things" with this.  Automation can be very unforgiving, and so I'd rather err on the side of diligence when it comes to code-merging by a robot-overlord :grin: 

I think the ***last*** thing any maintainer wants is for a "known-failing" change to get merged in because the robot wasn't paying attention correctly.  Even if it was just a flake (above), I'm glad nobody needed to discover this bug  3,000 commits down the road :disappointed_relieved:

I think we've got it sorted now with a proven workaround.  I'll give it one-more 'bors try' to confirm it's watching the 'success' status now...
bors try

okay, good.  I think the root-cause was simply a flake, but bors is doing the right thing now.  I'm going to un-block and attempt to have bors merge it, under my watchful pair of vision-orbs.
bors r+

As per usual, these cause much :confounded: and there's about nothing we can do other than re-run :disappointed:   I'm presently babysitting re-runs on the failures above, otherwise (at last) this is actually ready to merge.  It's up to you if you want to wait for all-green + no flakes or not.
Looks like a transient. Give it one more try. (I can pull that image locally).

Is there any visibility into bors? Any way to get it to include links, or verbose logs, or something to indicate why it thinks the build failed?

> something to indicate why it thinks the build failed?

Bors operates very simply, by merging queued PRs into either the 'trying' or 'staging' branch, then letting CI execute a post-merge (branch) run.  So the run can be interacted with simply by visiting the branch, going under 'commits', and clicking the status icon (red "X" or green check).   As a short-cut, you can click the status icon the right-side, next to the commit sha, on the line below where it says:

"bors bot added a commit that referenced this pull request ... ago"
Oh - that little message never even registered (for me) as anything but noise. Thank you!

@edsantiago Since BATS doesn't have a per-test flake-retry mechanism like ginkgo, I was thinking of adding one.  It's not a perfect fix, but should help, especially as more tests are added and/or the matrix expands.  Sound okay?

That's a super-complicated issue right now. Can you hold off for a little while? I've had a PR blocked now for months, #2036, that intends to solve this very issue. (It is blocked because right now the CI images don't install podman, they use, ugh, docker. I'm assuming that this will be fixed very soon, since we need podman to test on f31. As soon as that gets fixed, I can get back to my PR).

> they use, ugh, docker

Oh!  Wait!  No!  ***THIS*** PR fixes that, it's all podman here now.  Maybe I can rebase this onto #2036?

Yikes - I don't know, doesn't that get circular? TBH I don't even remember the state of my PR right now - it was passing in my VMs, but that was a long time ago. It definitely needs to be rebased and updated. Let me look into that right now.
It won't get circular if it's rebased, then I rebase this onto it.  When this PR merges, it will drag the others along with it all at once.
Within this PR, the commit-list will look awful, but it works out after merging.
Let's see if moving that whalesay image over to quay helps.

To address the broader question: @nalind there is a hodgepodge of images in `digest.bats`, from various different registries. Is it a deliberate choice to hit on many different registries? If not, could some of those be cleaned up so we are more robust in the face of registry failures? TIA.

@edsantiago good question.  @nalind my last commit adds quay to `registries.conf`, and I've manually copied whalesay over there.  Same for 'fedora-minimal', since docker.io also seems to be randomly breaking :confounded:

> To address the broader question: @nalind there is a hodgepodge of images in `digest.bats`, from various different registries. Is it a deliberate choice to hit on many different registries? If not, could some of those be cleaned up so we are more robust in the face of registry failures? TIA.

Hitting multiple registries isn't a goal there - we're using images in registries that we know have manifests in specific formats, to check that images in each of those formats can be successfully pulled and used as base images, and that the commit logic doesn't get tripped up by differences between them.

Back to the question I was trying to ask: @nalind is there any way we can change the list of images in this test, to (1) get more reliable registries (or at least not have to hit multiple ones), and (2) use smaller images, because the whale one is gigantic and hence more prone to network/disk failures?
@cevich I've pushed a new https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/2036 and have some confidence in it, but I don't think it will help with the whalesay problem.
Thanks @edsantiago taking a look to see how to integrate it here.
okay, shuffled some things around, now #2036 should be running...lessee whah happn...

> @nalind does that imply the tests depend on hard-coded references to metadata inside external content that we don't control?
> 
> Networking reliability aside, that makes me uncomfortable as it seems test-breakage would be both eventually-likely and unpredictable.

For references that use tags, yes, different images could be pushed to those tags, and that would almost certainly change their manifests.  I'm hoping that doesn't happen with these tags, which refer to older versions of the images.  References that use digests can't be changed out from under us, because the result would be an image whose reference would include a different digest.

> Back to the question I was trying to ask: @nalind is there any way we can change the list of images in this test, to (1) get more reliable
 registries (or at least not have to hit multiple ones), and (2) use smaller images, because the whale one is gigantic and hence more prone to network/disk failures?

To do that, we'd need to change the references to point to other images whose manifests exhibit the characteristics we're looking for.  Aside from that requirement, I'm not picky about which ones we use.  These were merely cases that I was aware of (IIRC, because they were mentioned in bug reports).
@nalind what are those characteristics? The tests in `digest.bats` don't actually seem to check for anything at all AFAICT.

>point to other images whose manifests exhibit the characteristics we're looking for. 

Thanks @nalind for explaining / confirming.

> hoping that doesn't happen

There's no forgiveness of in automation, even with infinite hope available :cry:  We've really got to exert control over both ends.  I'm happy to shove these special images somewhere static, where they can "never" be touched.  However, even that seems slightly fragile.  Is there a way to define these images + specific manifests in code, so they can be re-created if need-be?

Can we just take a step back and figure out what exactly is being tested? AFAICT the digest test simply does a pull-from-remote followed by a number of local push/commit steps. It doesn't actually check anything. @nalind if you help me understand what the purpose of this test is, I could try to (a) come up with better, safer, more representative images, and then (b) rewrite the test so it actually tests something.

@edsantiago we seem to be in much better shape now including your #2036 in the mix.  The only thing I'm seeing is that looks new is: https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5267044266409984?command=integration_test#L360

My intention was to clobber F29 here, once we got F31 up-n-going in libpod.  However, I'm not opposed to disabling it preemptively if that failure is a PITA to fix (the test seems to be working fine elsewhere).

> purpose of this test is, I could try to

Yeah!  That's what I was trying to say :smile:

> The only thing I'm seeing is that looks new is [...]
>[+1481s] not ok 286 images json test
>[+1481s] # #| Expected 30 lines of output, got 26

Interesting. It looks like `history` is getting lost. That's not happening on my test setup. Looking into it further now...

Root cause: too-old podman. 1.6.2 does not seem to handle image history.

I despise number-of-lines tests; so I'll try to rewrite it to something better. Sadly, we don't have `jq` in our test images, so I won't write anything that relies on it. But as a low-priority pretty-please, for some day, could you maybe add `jq` into the CI images so future tests can use it? TIA

> @nalind what are those characteristics? The tests in `digest.bats` don't actually seem to check for anything at all AFAICT.

We're checking that a given image can be pulled, used as a base image for a build, and that a new image that we build using that pulled image as a base can successfully be pushed to somewhere other than local storage, when the image is:
* a v2s1 image referenced using its digest
* a v2s1 image where one of the entries is a duplicate that gets discarded when it's converted to v2s2
* a v2s1 image referenced using its tag
* a v2s2 image referenced using its digest (incorrectly - this is also a v2s1 image, `alpine@sha256:25fd8fc1aefcc8ae46aae23daefcd7dcb97f676fa0bc72bb0bf7cfb75df4f22e` is a better candidate)
* a v2s2 image referenced using its tag (incorrectly - this is the same v2s1 image, `alpine:3.1` is a better candidate, though it's a manifest list now, so the test won't pass on non-amd64 systems without tweaks.
* a v2s2 image referenced using its repository name, with neither a tag nor a digest.  It's a manifest list, so the test won't pass on non-amd64 systems without tweaks.

>   could you maybe add jq into the CI images

Oh, that's actually really easy to do in buildah-CI since we're not caching VM images (they're built from scratch every damn time)...
@edsantiago done...added `jq` and disabled F29.
@cevich, thanks, our messages crossed in the ether. If you disabled f29, you should be able to rerun tests and have them pass (with or without rebaseing on my new force-push). Thank you!
Yep, rebasing anyway just to be sure since this is now a franken-PR :smile:

> @nalind, thank you, I'm trying to (ahem) digest all that. IIUC you are saying that those images are all hand-picked due to [certain characteristics], and that the test doesn't need to check those characteristics, it simply needs to guarantee that buildah doesn't crash when operating on them?

That, plus we make sure that we generate an image that can be pushed.  Some of the bug reports noted that we succeeded at committing a new image, but subsequently failed to push that image when logic in containers/image noticed, or was tripped up by, an error in how we'd constructed the data that we had generated at commit-time.

> I have one big question though:
> 
> > can successfully be pushed to somewhere other than local storage
> 
> AFAICT `digest.bats:fromreftest()` only pushes to local storage. Is that an oversight, or have I missed something?

Sorry, I was being imprecise.  By "local storage", I mean the storage managed by containers/storage.  For the purposes of testing the committing and pushing bits that these images previously tripped up (i.e., the logic that builds the new image's config blob and manifest), a `dir:` destination works as well as a `docker://` destination would, since in both cases we end up using the containers/image image-copying logic.
@nalind thank you, that helps me understand. Since tests are green (!) (!!!!), I'd like to move discussion of this aspect over to #2147.
@edsantiago @cevich @nalind Does that mean this PR is ready to merge?
@rhatdan I sure hope so but let's wait for @cevich to supervise the process
yes please.  hurry before the robots realize it's Friday!
Big thanks @edsantiago @nalind @rhatdan for help and patients with this effort.  Hopefully time will prove it was a worthwhilen investment..
bors r+
:-1: Rejected by too few approved reviews
bors r+
This PR was included in a batch that successfully built, but then failed to merge into master (it was a non-fast-forward update). It will be automatically retried.

Well...the good news is I did ***NOT*** need to do a single re-run of any test.  I believe that message from Bors (above) indicates that master changed during execution.  That could be caused by a non-bors entity merging a PR or otherwise changing the target branch HEAD.

# Build failed
  * cirrus-ci/success
bors retry
(timeout building a container image in a test)

I opened https://forum.bors.tech/t/bug-wildcard-status-ignores-1-match/438 regarding the wildcard false-positive problem discovered back here https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/2115#issuecomment-582069179

  * cirrus-ci/success
